ops.text: cc-guides artifact retirement procedure PINNED (recon lane): render ORPHANS artifacts, never deletes (render.go:278 area; proven by TestFullRenderRebuildsLockPruningRemovedTarget); lock prunes ONLY on a full no-arg render (scoped render Merges and cannot prune); no retire subcommand exists. Deleting a shared fragment while any consumer layout still references it = hard error (ErrUnknownFragment, exit 2) on any unpinned render — and the fleet runs DAILY ci-render jobs, so ordering is binding: delete consumer layouts fleet-wide FIRST, delete cc-skills capt-hook-*.toml fragments LAST, gated on zero remaining references via cc-skills tools/fleetlib gh.repos_with_file(".claude/fragments/.claude/capt-hook.toml/layout.toml") — the authoritative roster is the GitHub probe, not local clones. Per-repo procedure: rm -rf .claude/fragments/.claude/capt-hook.toml/; rm .claude/capt-hook.toml; cc-guides render (full, no args — prunes lock + drops unused source pins); cc-guides check; commit layout+file+lock together. Full render re-renders ALL repo artifacts (expected). Side finding: cc-context's .claude/capt-hook.toml is hand-authored (no layout, not in lock) — plain delete in Phase 2, no cc-guides mechanics. Local-clone layout count discrepancy (recon's shell sweep said 30, my python glob found 38) is moot — GitHub probe decides.
